DTR | Listen to a Short Exchange

LSE-MS6-L4-BR300-MSMATH6-ATA-AAD-03 | MS Math: Grade 6 | Algebraic Expressions | Artifact Analysis | African & African Diaspora Voices

The Repeating Design

Click 🎧 to listen.

Listen to a conversation between a girl and a boy. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once. After listening, you will answer a question.

Listen to the Conversation
QUESTION:

Why does the boy compare the cloth to what they are doing in math?

Choose the best answer. You may change your answer before submitting.

SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. The boy compares the cloth to math expressions. Use the words variable, coefficient, and equivalent expression to explain why his comparison works.

CA ELD Standards: PI.B.5 | PI.A.1 | CCSS.MATH.CONTENT.6.EE
